schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.

person.wealth_source: Omega Advisors hedge fund founder (1991-2018, converted to family office); prior 25-year career at Goldman Sachs, rose to chairman/CEO of Goldman Sachs Asset Management

net_worth.range_note: Multiple secondary sources cite Cooperman in the $3.2-$3.7B range as of late 2025 / early 2026. Gurufocus (Mar 2026) ~$3.61B; CelebsLives ~$3.6B; other estimates cluster around $3.2B. Forbes profile page could not be fetched directly (access blocked) but tertiary reporting consistently places him in this band. Prompt range target given as $2.5-3.5B; using $3.3B as central.

sources.publisher: Forbes profile (fetch blocked; reported via aggregators)

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth held primarily through Omega Advisors family office (converted from public-facing hedge fund in 2018). Highly liquid — cash, public securities, and hedge fund assets, not concentrated founder-equity. Apply 85% liquidity for 'giving capacity now' calculations (vs. 25% for Ellison's Oracle stake). This is the standard treatment for hedge fund principals.

pledges_and_announcements.pledged_amount_note: Giving Pledge = 'majority' of wealth (minimum 50%). Cooperman has publicly stated he plans to give away at least half in lifetime and put the rest into a foundation for his kids. Net worth at 2010 signing was ~$1.8B per Forbes, implying ~$900M-$1.8B pledge floor. He has also stated he wants to 'give it all away' in lifetime — watchable but not formally pledged.
pledges_and_announcements.status_source: Observable LTCFF 990-PF grants 2012-2025 sum to ~$230M plus multiple direct announced gifts (Hunter $25M, Columbia $25M, Saint Barnabas $100M, Cooperman College Scholars $25-50M, Jewish Community Foundation $20M) — implying $400M-$700M+ paid-to-date philanthropy on a ~$3.3B current net worth base. Approaching/exceeding 50% minimum pledge target if we include announced commitments.
pledge_letter_quotes: We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give. — Sir Winston Churchill (quoted)
A man's net worth is measured not by what he earns but rather what he gives away. — Talmud (quoted)
Toby and I feel it is our moral imperative to give others the opportunity to pursue the American dream by sharing our financial success.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Signed in 2010, the Giving Pledge's inaugural year. One of the smaller-net-worth Giving Pledgers, which makes the ratio-to-wealth more favorable than mega-billionaire peers. Cooperman has publicly stated an intent to 'give it all away' (Jewish Standard reporting) but formal Giving Pledge commitment is the standard majority-of-wealth. Has been one of the most publicly vocal defenders of billionaire philanthropy as a substitute for wealth tax.

pledges_and_announcements.quote_paraphrased: Cooperman has stated he will give away half of his wealth in his lifetime and the other half to his children to form a foundation as his legacy.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Prompt flags Cooperman's 'give away ALL' intent as watchable. Jewish Standard reporting indicates the more precise framing is: half in lifetime, half through a family foundation that continues giving after death. Functionally, 100% of wealth eventually goes to charitable purposes under this plan — but the downstream-foundation-for-kids model has historically been a structure where heirs retain de facto control and compensation, so this is not equivalent to a clean 100% charitable commitment. Watchable but not counted above 50% floor.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Cooperman's political footprint is modest compared to peer hedge fund billionaires (Griffin, Simons, Singer) and does not materially displace his observable charitable giving.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Low-confidence floor estimate from press. OpenSecrets donor-lookup not directly bulk-pulled in this research run. Historical record shows small donations across both parties ($2,300 Obama 2008, $2,100 McCain 2008, $1,000 Clinton 2016) indicating moderate-scale political participation. Has been vocal Republican-leaning commentator in recent years but no evidence of super-PAC mega-donations comparable to Ellison/Griffin. Needs FEC bulk pull to tighten.

sources.note: Donor lookup page exists; not bulk-parsed in this pass.
Characterizes Cooperman as having 'history of controversial political commentary' — primarily vocal opposition to wealth tax, not a major super-PAC donor.

foundations_active.name: Leon and Toby Cooperman Family Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (all seven trustees — Leon Cooperman, Toby Cooperman, Michael Cooperman, Wayne Cooperman, Jodi Cooperman, Courtney Cooperman, Anne Archer — receive $0 compensation per 2025 filing)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: Grants out in 2025 ($22.6M) vs. prior-year assets ($433.7M) = ~5.2%. RIGHT AT the 5% legal minimum across years. Foundation is accumulating faster than it is granting — 2012 assets were $147M, 2025 assets are $581M (4x growth) while grants_out grew only 3x. Watch for LOW_PAYOUT flag: 2022 payout was ~5.2%, 2021 was ~5.4%, 2020 was ~5.4% — consistently at floor, not above.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED (would require parsing grant recipients list; ProPublica summary page does not disclose recipients)

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified in summary-level review. Inside Philanthropy reporting suggests Cooperman is known for direct named gifts to institutions (hospitals, universities) — low DAF-usage profile compared to peers.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified. Omega Advisors is the family office (for-profit investment management), not a philanthropic vehicle.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. No public reporting of offshore activity.

red_flags.evidence: September 2016: SEC charged Cooperman and Omega Advisors with insider trading in Atlas Pipeline Partners (APL) securities based on material nonpublic information. Settled May 2017 for $4.9M without admitting wrongdoing; subject to onsite compliance monitoring through 2022. Relevant to philanthropic credibility only insofar as it contextualizes wealth origination — does not negate giving.

LTCFF consistently pays out at or near the 5% legal minimum. Foundation assets 4x since 2012 ($147M → $581M) but grants_out only 3x ($7M → $22.6M). Foundation is compounding faster than it grants, implying the endowment is being built up rather than deployed. At 5% payout on $581M, annual grants are ~$29M — fine for a small foundation but not commensurate with the rhetoric of 'giving it all away'.

All seven LTCFF trustees are family members (Cooperman spouse, two sons, daughters-in-law, granddaughter). Cooperman has publicly stated plan is half-to-charity-in-lifetime, half-to-foundation-controlled-by-kids. Under the Giving Pledge's 'majority of wealth' rule this qualifies, but the family-foundation-in-perpetuity model is structurally weaker than direct deployment — heirs control the giving in perpetuity, which compounds the LOW_PAYOUT risk over generations.

red_flags_not_present: NO_OBSERVABLE_GIVING — $230M+ documented from LTCFF alone over 14 years.
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP — all LTCFF trustees receive zero comp.
PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED — On track or functionally fulfilled at current giving pace vs. 50% of ~$3.3B floor.
POLITICAL_HEAVY — modest political footprint, does not dominate charitable giving.
DAF_TRANSFERS — no evidence of DAF-intermediary routing.

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(LTCFF 990-PF grants_out 2012-2025) = $230.4M. Excludes: Columbia 2011 ($25M, pre-ProPublica window, likely via LTCFF), Hunter 2014 ($25M, likely inside LTCFF 2013-2018), Saint Barnabas 2014 ($25M, likely inside LTCFF), Saint Barnabas 2021 ($100M, likely multi-year pledge with partial payment via LTCFF), Cooperman College Scholars 2015 ($25M, likely inside LTCFF), Birthright 2010 ($5M, pre-window), Jewish Community Foundation 2024 ($20M, likely inside LTCFF 2024). Bigger personal-direct gifts outside LTCFF cannot be ruled out — so $230M is LOWER BOUND.
rollup.observable_giving_upper_bound_method: If major announced gifts were partly/wholly from personal assets outside LTCFF: LTCFF $230M + 50% of $225M announced-gifts set + $100M Saint Barnabas pledge-to-date (~50%) = ~$400-580M plausible upper bound. Jewish Standard reporting characterizes lifetime giving as 'nearing the billion-dollar mark' — consistent with this upper bound if personal gifts are additive.
observable_giving_todo: Pull LTCFF pre-2012 990-PFs (1982-2011) via IRS SOI — foundation has been active since 1981, 30 years of pre-ProPublica data exists.
Verify whether $100M Saint Barnabas 2021 gift is disbursing as multi-year pledge via LTCFF or was paid separately from personal Omega Advisors assets.
Verify whether Hunter $25M 2014, Columbia $25M 2011, Saint Barnabas $25M 2014 were LTCFF-routed or personal.
FEC bulk pull for Leon Cooperman political giving total 2010-2025.
Parse LTCFF grant recipients to check for DAF routing.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.85 × $3.3B = $2.805B; tenure cap saturated (20 years). Expected = $280.5M. Cooperman's observable floor of $230M is 82% of this norm; upper bound ~$580M exceeds it 2x.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Cooperman's public profile is the OPPOSITE of anonymous — nearly every major gift is named (Cooperman Library, Cooperman Barnabas Medical Center, Cooperman College Scholars, Cooperman Scholarship). Anonymous uplift bounded low. Apply 0.2% of liquid wealth = $5.6M, rounded up to $50M to allow for direct personal-asset gifts outside LTCFF that are simply not in press coverage.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Cooperman is publicly Jewish; multiple significant gifts to Jewish causes (Birthright, Jewish Community Foundation of MetroWest, Jewish Federation). Jewish philanthropic giving is typically visible rather than hidden (unlike churches). Small uplift bounded at $20M for synagogue-level giving not captured in 990 recipient data.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Cooperman's stated plan: half to charity in lifetime, half to family foundation controlled by heirs after death. The family-foundation half (~$1.5B at current NW) is committed to charitable purposes in principle but deployed at heir-controlled pace — historically a very uncertain pathway. We set an uncertainty envelope of $1.5B representing the eventual-charitable-deployment portion of the family-controlled estate, but DO NOT include this in tier calculation. Flag for downstream Tier A/B/C scoring.

rollup.tier_rule: Observable $230M / expected $281M = 82%. Upper bound $580M / expected $281M = 207%. Even at the strict lower bound Cooperman is within spitting distance of the 10% formal norm. Add credibly-named announced gifts ($225M+) and he clears the norm 2x. He is a Tier C 'on track' giver — not a Bill Gates / MacKenzie Scott Tier VERIFIED_HIGH (that requires >5x expected), but meaningfully above the 'signed but hasn't delivered' cohort. Red flags (SEC settlement, payout-at-floor, dynasty structure) lower confidence but do not reverse the classification.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Cooperman is the canonical example of 'Giving Pledge signer who is actually giving.' Net worth is small ($3.3B vs. Ellison's $227B), which makes the pledge target reachable within an ordinary human lifetime. Public persona as philanthropy-defender-in-chief aligns with observable disbursements. The structural risk is dynasty-foundation compounding rather than lifetime deployment — watch LTCFF payout rate over the next decade.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md. Low score reflects solid observable giving ratio, on-track pledge, modest but not zero red flags.

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Leon and Toby Cooperman Family Foundation (Boca Raton, FL) + Omega Advisors family office + Cooperman College Scholars — document delivery to foundation trustees directly.
